description: Every theme raised in the meeting maps to an existing JIRA ticket. The Gap Detector must catch all three as duplicates rather than presenting them as new stories.

deterministic_metrics.name: acceptance_criteria_well_formed
observations: 3/3 stories have AC count in range

deterministic_metrics.name: expected_duplicates_found
observations: Expected duplicate (['stock', 'badge'] ↔ NS-412) not found at confidence ≥ medium
Expected duplicate (['pos', 'offline'] ↔ NS-101) not found at confidence ≥ medium

reasons.ac_quality: All acceptance criteria are concrete, testable, and follow Given/When/Then structure with observable outcomes (badge visibility, email content, offline transaction completion).
reasons.priority_justification: No priority_rationale fields exist in any story despite the guardrail flagging this weakness; priorities appear assigned without documented business justification.
reasons.story_granularity: Stories represent single, well-scoped behaviors (fix badge accuracy, add tier info to email, enable offline cash) though ST-03 is slightly broad with both offline detection and sync concerns.
reasons.tag_accuracy: Tags are appropriate and drawn from a consistent technical vocabulary (mobile-app, ecommerce, pos, loyalty, inventory, offline-mode, payments) that accurately reflects each story's scope.
reasons.conflict_reasoning: No conflicts are flagged, yet ST-03 should cite C-13 (mandate for offline cash sales) and C-15 (forbid offline card sales) as directly constraining its acceptance criteria, making the absence of conflict annotations a miss.

llm_judge.overall_comment: The synthesis demonstrates strong technical writing with excellent testable acceptance criteria and appropriate story sizing, but completely omits priority rationale fields that would justify the High/Medium assignments. The constraint analysis is incomplete: ST-03 directly implements C-13 and must comply with C-15, yet these are not surfaced as conflicts or dependencies, suggesting the conflict-detection logic may only flag violations rather than relevant constraint applications.
